macro_summary: Equity markets drifted flat-to-slightly-higher Thursday with SPY closing at $701.66 (+0.14%) and QQQ at $640.47 (+0.33%), extending the recovery streak as VIX fell a further 1.27% to 17.94 — its lowest reading in over a month. Overnight, per Yahoo Finance, futures climbed on news that Israel and Lebanon agreed to a 10-day ceasefire, calming some of the regional instability fears that have weighed on sentiment for weeks; this is a new de-escalation signal at the Middle East perimeter, separate from the ongoing Iran-Hormuz situation. INTC earnings are due April 24 (7 days out) and represent the next near-term binary event for the semis cluster, while AMZN, GOOG, and MSFT all report April 30.
commodities_note: WTI pulled back 1.91% to $89.98/bbl — notably the benchmark data shows $89.98 while the macro_news article from OilPrice.com references WTI at $93.62 intraday, reflecting a data timing discrepancy; the pipeline closing price of $89.98 is used as canonical. Gold eased 0.78% to $4,809.90/troy oz after its recent all-time high run — still historically elevated and well above any near-term moving average support. The dollar firmed slightly (DXY +0.33% to 98.26) and US 10-year yields crept up 0.63% to 4.31%, holding above the 4.20% NIM-compression reset threshold for Singapore banks. The Hormuz blockade persists at day 5, per OilPrice.com, with cargo rerouting adding roughly 10 extra days to Central Asian shipments and pushing ECA inflation to 8.1% in February 2026 per the IMF — structural cost pressure even as spot WTI softens. Gold remains near all-time highs but GLD is well extended above its 50-day average, making a fresh entry unattractive at current levels.

U11_SI.signal_rationale: Signal changed from WATCH to ACCUMULATE — all eight mechanical gates pass and no specific ticker-level thesis deterioration exists. UOB sits just 0.72% above its declining 50-day average of SGD 37.23 with RSI at a neutral 58.9, placing it squarely in the entry zone. The invalidation level is the 50-day average at SGD 37.23 — a close below confirms the recent rally has failed. Headline R:R to the nearest observed resistance at SGD 39.49 appears 7.6:1, but the tight stop (0.7% below price) means normal intraday volatility could trigger it; the actionable sizing R:R using structural support at SGD 35.92 is 1.3:1 — use SGD 35.92 as the mental stop for position sizing. NIM compression from US10Y holding at 4.31% (above the 4.20% reset threshold) remains the standing headwind per the Singapore bank thesis, and MAS maintained its S$NEER appreciation pace in April, compounding pressure. Volume is below average, which argues for a smaller starter position. ACCUMULATE — starter position. Bottom line: UOB is the cleanest technical entry in the Singapore bank cluster right now — fairly priced given its yield and book value — but the rate environment hasn't turned yet, so size conservatively and set your mental stop near SGD 35.92. PAPER TRADE — under evaluation.

NVDA.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at CAUTION. NVDA has surged 8.54% above its 50-day average of $182.74 with RSI at 68.9 — the pipeline's hard block applies and no trend exception is met (only 6 sessions above the average, SMA50 not rising). The fundamental picture is genuinely strong: per NVDA's Q4 FY2026 earnings call, Blackwell Ultra ramp is underway with Q1 FY2027 guidance of $78B implying accelerating shipment velocity, and at 17.7x forward PE with a PEG of 0.81, NVDA trades at a 25.6% discount to the semis cluster median — the valuation is not the problem. The issue is purely technical: at these extension levels, R:R is 0.9:1 (invalidation at $182.74, upside to $212.17) with observed support at $177.41 below. Volume has not confirmed the move, adding fragility. Bottom line: NVDA's AI thesis is intact and the valuation is reasonable, but the stock has run too far, too fast to enter now — wait for a pullback toward the $177-183 support band before re-evaluating.

AMD.entry_block: BLOCKED: +32.1% above 50-day SMA (>20% extreme extension). No trend exception applies at this level — entry requires a meaningful pullback regardless of trend duration.

risk_reward.invalidation_reason: 50-day SMA -- break below confirms rally failure

AMD.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at HOLD. AMD surged 7.73% today and is now 32.15% above its 50-day average of $210.57 with RSI at 80.2 — a textbook extreme extension that triggers the hard block regardless of thesis strength. Per AMD's IR press release (February 24, 2026), the Meta GPU supply framework for MI450 Instinct deployments (up to 6GW, first shipments H2 2026) is confirmed but remains a framework ceiling, not a firm purchase order — revenue is contingent on MI450 benchmark milestones. The AMD-Meta deal validates AMD as a credible NVDA alternative at hyperscale, and the 25.5x forward PE with a 0.77 PEG is attractive for 34.1% revenue growth. However, at 32% above the 50-day average with the SMA itself not rising, entering now means buying into a parabolic move where the risk of mean reversion is acute. R:R is 0.2:1 to the nearest resistance at $292.17 vs. 24.3% downside to SMA50 invalidation. Bottom line: AMD is doing exactly what the Meta deal thesis predicted, but at these levels you're paying the premium upfront — wait for a pullback to the $224-235 zone before the setup becomes actionable.

INTC.sma50_warning: SMA50 is 29% below price — pullback to SMA50 would require a crash-level move. Use shorter-term support levels for entry guidance instead.
INTC.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at HOLD. Intel has exploded 40.97% above its 50-day average with RSI at 78.2 — the extreme extension hard block is absolute. Do not use the 50-day average as an entry reference; it is 29% below price and a pullback there would be a crash-level event. The 18A node thesis remains unproven — as of April 2026, there are no confirmed external foundry customers, only internal Panther Lake products. Critically, INTC reports earnings in approximately 7 days (April 24), making any directional position here a binary earnings bet on top of an already extreme technical setup. Forward PE of 65.3x at a 174.4% premium to the semis cluster median is unjustifiable without revenue growth — revenue is actually declining 4.1% YoY and FCF yield is negative at -1.31%. Bottom line: Intel's run is impressive but the stock is priced for perfection with no proven foundry revenue — avoid until earnings clarify the 18A trajectory and the technical extension normalizes toward the $40-50 zone.

risk_reward.invalidation_reason: 50-day SMA -- break below confirms rally failure

ASML.signal_rationale: Signal changed from CAUTION to BUY — ASML pulled back 4.54% today to $1,410.83, landing just 1.04% above its rising 50-day average of $1,396.37, which puts it squarely in the entry zone. The invalidation level is the 50-day average at $1,396.37 — a close below signals the setup has failed. Tight stop warning: the invalidation is only 1.0% below current price, within normal daily volatility; use structural support at $1,314.33 (6.8% below) as the mental stop for position sizing — the headline R:R of 5.7:1 is distorted by this tight stop, while the sizing R:R to structural support is 0.8:1, meaning the true risk-adjusted setup is less favorable than the headline suggests. Volume confirms the move. ASML holds a literal monopoly on extreme ultraviolet lithography machines — every advanced chip fab on earth (TSMC, Samsung, Intel) must buy from ASML, making it the infrastructure layer of the AI buildout regardless of which GPU architecture wins. High-NA EUV customer ramp for Intel's 14A node is expected H2 2026 per ASML's Q4 2025 earnings call, adding a near-term revenue leg. At 29.5x forward PE with a PEG of 2.41, valuation is modestly above cluster median and slightly expensive for growth — the PEG flags caution — but the monopoly position justifies a premium. Per GuruFocus, ASML's premium to US peers has shrunk to a decade low despite raised sales guidance. Bottom line: ASML at the 50-day average is the best risk-reward entry on the watchlist today — the world's only EUV supplier at a decade-low relative valuation. Size conservatively given the tight stop; the real risk level is $1,314.

MU.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at HOLD. Micron has recovered strongly but sits 12.96% above its 50-day average of $404.78 with RSI at 63.4, triggering the hard block. The inference-driven HBM memory demand thesis remains intact — Micron's forward PE of just 4.6x against 196.3% revenue growth is genuinely anomalous (the PEG of 0.27 appears low even accounting for the cyclical earnings inflection; note the divergence between 4.6x forward and 21.5x trailing PE signals an expected earnings surge, not a valuation error). Analyst consensus per multiple sources projects strong earnings growth, though Micron's own management commentary on memory price increases carries inherent seller bias and should be treated as directionally informative, not gospel. Nearest resistance is $471.14 against 11.5% downside to invalidation — R:R is 0.3:1. Bottom line: Micron is arguably the cheapest quality name on the watchlist relative to its growth trajectory, but it needs to pull back to the $400-420 zone before the entry math works.

risk_reward.invalidation_reason: 50-day SMA -- break below confirms rally failure

TSM.signal_rationale: Signal changed from CAUTION to BUY — TSMC pulled back 3.61% today to $363.35, settling 2.98% above its rising 50-day average of $352.82 with RSI at a neutral 55.4 and volume confirming the move. The invalidation level is the 50-day average at $352.82 — a close below signals rally failure. R:R is 1.5:1 to nearest resistance at $378.93 (observed), which is below the 2:1 threshold for ACCUMULATE, keeping the signal at BUY rather than ACCUMULATE. TSMC is the physical bottleneck of the AI buildout: CoWoS packaging capacity is fully booked and scaling from ~80K to 120-130K wafers/month by end-2026, with NVDA reportedly booking over 50% of that capacity. Per Zacks, both TSMC and ASML posted strong growth in the latest quarter, with the CEO's commentary on AI demand signaling continued confidence. At 19.2x forward PE with a PEG of 1.31, TSMC is modestly below the semis cluster median — not cheap, but not stretched for the volume and pricing power it commands. Support below sits at $330.99 (8.9% down). Bottom line: TSMC at the 50-day average with confirmed volume is a clean entry for the AI infrastructure thesis — it doesn't matter which GPU wins if every advanced chip runs through TSMC's fabs. Entry at current levels, stop on a close below $352.

AMZN.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at HOLD. Amazon remains 16.74% above its 50-day average of $213.89 with RSI at 76.2 — the hard block stands. Per Reuters, NiSource signed new energy agreements with Amazon to accelerate power delivery to its data center sites, reinforcing the scale of AWS infrastructure build. The $200B AI capex program (per AMZN's 2025 earnings call and 10-K) remains the dominant thesis, with Trainium custom chip development reducing NVDA dependence. At 26.6x forward PE with a PEG of 1.91, valuation is reasonable but not cheap, and analyst consensus projects only 5% earnings growth — well below the revenue growth rate — reflecting capex absorption. AMZN reports April 30 (13 days out); AWS and capex guidance will be the signal validators. Bottom line: Amazon's AI infrastructure story is compelling but the stock needs to pull back to the $225-235 zone — and the April 30 earnings print is the next real catalyst to watch.

GOOG.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at HOLD. Google sits 8.45% above its 50-day average of $306.85 with RSI at 70.0, maintaining the hard block. Per Simply Wall St., Alphabet is in discussions with the U.S. Pentagon to deploy Google Gemini AI models for classified defense applications — a potential new revenue vertical if confirmed, though the pipeline cites no formal announcement and this should be treated as reportedly in early talks. The TurboQuant algorithm disruption risk (Day 22) remains a latent unvalidated threat — Q1 earnings on April 30 will be the first real test. TPU v7 rental momentum (Meta, Anthropic, Apple per The Information) is the strongest near-term revenue leg. PEG of 2.28 at 24.8x forward PE flags as moderately expensive for 18% revenue growth. Bottom line: Google's AI ecosystem is deepening but the stock is extended and carries the TurboQuant uncertainty — wait for the April 30 earnings print and a pullback toward $308-319 before entering.

MSFT.signal_rationale: Signal changed from CAUTION to ACCUMULATE — all eight mechanical gates pass and no specific ticker-level thesis deterioration exists. Microsoft sits 7.23% above its 50-day average of $391.91 with RSI at 70.8 — technically outside the ideal entry zone, but the mechanical gates clear because the pipeline's signal is CAUTION with caution_source base_scorer qualifying, R:R is 2.4:1 to the structural target at $488.02 (observed), and VIX is below 30. The nearest resistance at $422.72 is only 0.6% above — note this tight upside: the meaningful R:R ceiling uses $488.02 as the target, not the immediate $422.72 resistance. The invalidation level is the 50-day average at $391.91. The OpenAI concentration risk is the standing thesis caveat: approximately 45% of MSFT's $625B commercial RPO ties to OpenAI, and Azure AI revenue is partially circular (Microsoft invests in OpenAI → OpenAI spends Azure compute credits → Microsoft books it as revenue). The cleaner demand signal is non-OpenAI Azure RPO (~$350B, +28% YoY). Per Simply Wall St., Stellantis signed a five-year collaboration to move global digital infrastructure onto Azure — the kind of arm's-length enterprise win the thesis needs. At 22.2x forward PE with a PEG of 1.33, Microsoft trades 13.6% below the BigTech cluster median — reasonable for its growth and moat. MSFT reports April 30; focus on non-OpenAI RPO and Azure ex-OpenAI commentary as the thesis validators. ACCUMULATE — starter position. Entry zone $390-420, invalidation at $391.91 (50-day average), R:R 2.4:1 to $488. Bottom line: Microsoft is fairly priced for a cloud and AI business with genuine enterprise momentum — size small ahead of the April 30 earnings binary, and the key thing to watch is whether Azure growth holds up without the OpenAI credit boost. PAPER TRADE — under evaluation.

AVGO.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at HOLD. Broadcom is exactly 20.38% above its 50-day average of $331.02, triggering the extreme extension hard block. Per Simply Wall St., Meta announced an expanded multi-year, multi-generation partnership with Broadcom to co-develop custom MTIA AI accelerator chips and Ethernet networking through at least 2029, with an initial commitment of over one gigawatt of custom compute capacity — this is a confirmed catalyst from a company announcement, and it has driven an 8-day winning streak. However, at 22.1x forward PE with a PEG of 0.86 and a blended hardware-plus-software model (VMware adds recurring revenue), Broadcom at these levels offers R:R of 0.2:1. Nearest resistance at $401.39 is only 0.7% above — essentially at price. Bottom line: Broadcom's custom ASIC position is the strongest in the AI stack, but this is a stock to buy on pullbacks — wait for a retrace toward the $350-370 zone before the setup makes sense.

LITE.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at CAUTION. LITE surged 8.68% today but remains 28.23% above its 50-day average at $695.02 — the extreme extension hard block is absolute. Per NVIDIA's investor relations press release (March 2, 2026), the confirmed $2B strategic investment in Lumentum for AI optics R&D and US manufacturing buildout is the core thesis catalyst, along with a new 240,000-square-foot Greensboro facility for indium phosphide-based optical devices. This is a legitimate structural catalyst — co-packaged optics address the bandwidth bottleneck for GPU clusters beyond 100,000 GPUs. However, at 56.3x forward PE at a 136.6% premium to the semis cluster median, and with FCF yield effectively zero (-0.03%), the valuation embeds significant execution assumptions. The PEG of 0.17 appears anomalous given the 56.3x forward PE — verify earnings growth estimates before citing this as a valuation argument. R:R is 0.4:1. Bottom line: The NVDA partnership is real and strategically significant, but LITE needs a meaningful pullback — toward the $700-750 zone — before the risk-reward becomes attractive.

PLTR.entry_block: CAUTION: forward P/E of 77x (>40x). Requires explicit justification of why earnings growth supports this multiple — default to WATCH unless thesis is clearly articulated with specific catalysts and timeline.

PLTR.signal_rationale: Signal changed from CAUTION to ACCUMULATE — all eight mechanical gates pass. PLTR sits just 0.39% below its 50-day average of $143.31 with RSI at 48.9, placing it at the cleanest technical entry point on the watchlist. The pipeline's R:R headlines at 5.0:1 but this is distorted by an extremely tight stop (invalidation at $142.34, just 0.3% below price) — normal intraday moves could trigger this before the thesis is tested. The actionable sizing R:R using structural support at $136.30 is 0.3:1, which is unfavorable. However, the R:R gate passes on the headline 5.0:1 ratio (rr_distorted is false per pipeline), so the gate mechanics clear — but position sizing must account for the tight stop reality. The forward PE of 76.7x at a 198.4% premium to the BigTech cluster median is the fundamental concern: this multiple requires Palantir's $7.18-7.20B 2026 revenue guide (+61% YoY, per DoD press release and analyst notes) to materialize. The $10B Army IDIQ framework ceiling (not firm revenue) and confirmed $448M Navy ShipOS deal support the defense AI thesis, with Rosenblatt raising its price target to $200 and Daiwa upgrading to Buy with a $180 target — though analyst upgrades carry their own biases. The Iran war environment structurally benefits defense AI spending. ACCUMULATE — starter position. Entry zone $136-143, invalidation at $136.30 (structural support — treat the $142.34 level as the trigger watch, not the position-sizing stop), R:R to $148.21 resistance. Bottom line: Palantir at its 50-day average is the right setup for a starter position in defense AI, but the valuation is very demanding — keep this a small position and watch the $136 level as your real line in the sand. PAPER TRADE — under evaluation.

singapore.summary: The Singapore bank cluster remains structurally impaired at day 35 of NIM compression, with US10Y at 4.31% — still above the 4.20% reset threshold — and MAS maintaining its S$NEER appreciation pace. The key divergence today is U11 upgrading to ACCUMULATE as the only cluster member in the entry zone, while O39 stays hard-blocked at CAUTION after a 5.67% extension above its 50-day average.
singapore.key_development: U11 (UOB) upgraded to ACCUMULATE as the sole cluster member at a clean technical entry, offering a starter-position opportunity while the broader rate reset remains pending.

semiconductors.summary: SOXX gained 1.11% to $405.95, with the cluster bifurcating sharply: ASML and TSM both returned to BUY signals on post-earnings pullbacks to their 50-day averages, while AMD and INTC remain in extreme extension (32% and 41% above their averages respectively). NVDA stays blocked at CAUTION, 8.5% above its average with RSI near 69. The foundry names are the actionable entries; the mega-cap momentum plays are not.
semiconductors.thesis_status: Bifurcated — foundry names at entry, mega-cap momentum overextended
semiconductors.key_development: ASML's 4.54% pullback today brought it back to the 50-day average with confirmed volume — the first BUY-grade entry in the semis cluster since the Hormuz shock began.

big_tech.summary: All four BigTech members remain above their 50-day averages, with MSFT upgraded to ACCUMULATE after the pipeline's mechanical gates cleared — though the OpenAI credit circularity risk and the April 30 earnings binary argue for starter-only sizing. AMZN and GOOG are hard-blocked (16.7% and 8.45% above their averages) with the same April 30 earnings catalyst as the next real signal validator for the cluster.
big_tech.thesis_status: Extended — MSFT the sole entry candidate, rest blocked
big_tech.key_development: MSFT's Stellantis five-year Azure deal (per Simply Wall St.) adds an arm's-length enterprise win that partially offsets OpenAI revenue circularity concerns ahead of the April 30 earnings print.

interconnected.entry_note: Relevant as a cross-reference only; revisit if TSM ADR breaks above $378.93 resistance with volume, confirming CoWoS utilization thesis.

interconnected.name: Marvell Technology
interconnected.reason: Competing custom ASIC designer alongside AVGO for hyperscaler inference chips — if AVGO's Meta partnership expands, Marvell's share of remaining hyperscaler ASIC contracts (Amazon Trainium, Microsoft) becomes more visible.
interconnected.entry_note: Becomes actionable if Marvell pulls back 8-10% from current levels concurrent with an AMZN capex guidance beat on April 30 confirming Trainium ramp.

interconnected.name: Arista Networks
interconnected.reason: Primary beneficiary of hyperscaler datacenter networking buildout — directly linked to AMZN and GOOG capex guidance on April 30 as AI clusters require ultra-low-latency Ethernet switching.
interconnected.entry_note: Revisit after AMZN and GOOG April 30 earnings if AWS and Google Cloud capex guidance exceeds $60B combined for 2026, signaling continued network infrastructure demand.

interconnected.name: SK Hynix
interconnected.reason: Leading HBM3E memory supplier — directly competes with and complements MU's HBM ramp; SK Hynix's allocation splits with NVDA are the clearest read-through for MU's market share trajectory.
interconnected.entry_note: Monitor SK Hynix quarterly results (next expected late April) — if HBM3E ASP holds above prior quarter, revisit MU for a potential signal upgrade from HOLD.

active_risks: Iran-Hormuz blockade (Day 5): US Navy blockade persists; WTI at $89.98 down from $104.91 spike but logistics rerouting costs structural, adding ~10 days to Central Asian cargo routes per OilPrice.com/RFE/RL.

US-China trade truce (tactical): Per Coface, tariffs reduced from 41% to 31% on Chinese imports; reciprocal 24% duties suspended to November 2026; rare earth controls postponed — moderate relief, not resolution.
Israel-Lebanon 10-day ceasefire: Per Yahoo Finance, overnight agreement calmed instability fears; futures rallied — new de-escalation signal but fragile and time-limited.
Fed hawkish pause: US10Y at 4.31%, above the 4.20% Singapore bank NIM reset threshold; rate structure pressuring tech multiples and EM assets.
geopolitical.market_impact: The Hormuz disruption's direct commodity impact is fading (WTI -$15 from spike) but structural logistics costs remain embedded, keeping energy-import economies under inflation pressure — this compounds NIM headwinds for Singapore banks (D05, U11) and margin pressure for ASML and TSM via shipping reroutes. The Israel-Lebanon ceasefire provides a sentiment lift for risk assets broadly but has not altered PLTR's defense AI demand thesis, which is driven by the Iran conflict and broader Pentagon AI spending cycle.
geopolitical.new_since_yesterday: Israel-Lebanon 10-day ceasefire announced overnight (per Yahoo Finance), lifting equity futures and adding a partial geopolitical de-escalation signal not present yesterday.
geopolitical.portfolio_action: Selective entry on the two foundry names that have pulled back to their 50-day averages (ASML, TSM); hold cash on mega-cap BigTech and extended semis until April 30 earnings clarify capex conviction.

events_this_week.event: Intel (INTC) earnings — 18A node update and revenue trajectory
Microsoft (MSFT) earnings — Azure ex-OpenAI growth and non-OpenAI RPO trajectory are the key metrics
Alphabet (GOOG) earnings — TPU v7 revenue contribution and TurboQuant demand impact first real test
Amazon (AMZN) earnings — AWS capex guidance and Trainium ramp commentary
DBS (D05.SI) earnings — NIM trajectory and guidance on rate sensitivity
Non-Farm Payrolls (April) — labor market strength vs. Fed rate path implications
AMD earnings — MI450 framework shipment update and data center revenue confirmation
Lumentum (LITE) earnings — first NVDA optics partnership revenue visibility
NVIDIA (NVDA) earnings — Blackwell Ultra production volumes and Q2 FY2027 guidance

portfolio_snapshot.overall_stance: Selectively constructive — foundry entries open, mega-cap extended
portfolio_snapshot.risk_posture: April 30 earnings triple-header (MSFT, GOOG, AMZN) is the single most important risk event; capex guidance misses could re-trigger BigTech CAUTION signals and drag the semis cluster with them.
